figures.caption: Fig. 1. (Color online) Tsai-type cluster of the Au-SM-Tb consists of (a) cluster center, (b) dodecahedron, (c) icosahedron, (d) icosidodecahedron, and (e) defect rhombic triacontahedron with Au (yellow), Au/SM (green), and Tb (gray). The frame box is the unit cell of the (expanded) unit cell of the 1/1 AC Au-SM-Tb. (f) The local configuration of atoms surrounding the Tb atom in the Tsai-type cluster. The angle θ is defined between the magnetic easy axis J for the CEF ground state and the pseudo 5-fold axis (z axis).

Table I. Characteristics of each magnetic order realized in the 1/1 AC. The symbol in the 1st column denotes the magnetic state shown in Fig. 4 or Fig. 5. The magnetic propagation vector q = 2π a (hkl), the magnetic space group in the OG notation, the independent magnetic position, degeneracy, the total magnetic moment vector per icosahedron JIC, the total chirality χT, and topological charge On the 6th and 7th columns, the states with all the components of JIC and χ T having opposite sign are energetically degenerate. The IC1 (IC2) is the icosahedron located at the corner (center) of the unit cell in Fig. 4 and Fig. 5. The lattice constant a is a = 14.725 Å for cubic unit cell (i.e., the side length of a cube in Fig. 4 and Fig. 5).22)
Fig. 8. (Color online) The non-collinear ferrimagnetic state for θ = 40◦ on the icosahedron denoted by the blue-square symbol in Fig. 5(b).
Fig. 6. (Color online) The non-collinear ferrimagnetic state for θ = 10◦ on the icosahedron denoted by the brown-diamond symbol in Fig. 5(a).
Fig. 7. (Color online) The anisotropy-angle θ dependence of |χT | (left axis) and |JIC| (right axis) in the magnetic state shown in Fig. 5(a).

Fig. 17. (Color online) The anisotropy-angle θ dependence of |χT µ | (µ = x, y, z) (left axis) and the component of |JIC,µ | (µ = x, y, z) in the magnetic state shown in Fig. 5(g). The vertical dashed line indicates θ = θ0 + 90◦ (see text).

Fig. 2. (Color online) The nearest-neighbor (N.N.) interactions J1 (solid line) and next-nearest-neighbor (N.N.N.) interactions J2 (dashed line) for (a) the intra icosahedron and (b) the inter icosahedron are illustrated. In (b), the frame box represents the unit cell of the bcc lattice of the 1/1 AC with the lattice constant a.

Fig. 11. (Color online) The anisotropy-angle θ dependence of |χT µ | (µ = x, y, z) (left axis) and the component of |JIC,µ | (µ = x, y, z) (right axis) in the non-collinear ferrimagnetic state. The vertical dashed line indicates θ = θ0 (see text).
Fig. 9. (Color online) The anisotropy-angle θ dependence of χT z (left axis) and |JIC| (right axis) in the magnetic state shown in Fig. 5(b).

Fig. 4. (Color online) The antiferromagnetic orders on the icosahedrons located at the center and the corner of the bcc unit cell in the 1/1 AC. (a) The anti-hedgehog state at the center icosahedron and the hedgehog state at the corner icosahedron with the anisotropy angle θ = 0◦. (b) The anti-whirling state at the center icosahedron and the whirling state at the corner icosahedron with the anisotropy angle θ = 90◦.

Fig. 3. (Color online) (a) The ground-state phase diagram in the 1/1 AC (a) for J1 = 0 (top) and in the plane of J2/J1 and the magnetic anisotropy angle θ (◦) (lower panel). The open symbol denotes the ferrimagnetic order where the magnetic states on the icosahedrons located at the center and the corner of the bcc unit cell are distributed uniformly. The filled symbol denotes the antiferromagnetic order where the magnetic states on the icosahedrons located at the center and the corner of the bcc unit cell are distributed in the antiferromagnetic alignment. The vertical dashed lines at θ = 0◦, 90◦, and 180◦ are guides for the eyes. (b) The enlargement for 0 ≤ J2/J1 ≤ 1. In (a) and (b), the pink regions denote the ordered states with the topological charge |n| = 1 on the icosahedron. In (a), the blue region denotes the ordered state with the topological charge |n| = 3 on the icosahedron.

Fig. 19. (Color online) (a) The magnetic field dependence of the magnetization m, the topological charge |n|, and the total chirality χT z for J1 = 1 and J2 = 8 at θ = 90◦ . (b) Magnetic state at the center and corner icosahedrons in the unit cell of the bcc lattice for H > HM. (c) The energetically degenerate state with the magnetic state in (b) for H > HM. In (b) and (c), the number labels the rare-earth site on the icosahedron.

Fig. 18. (Color online) (a) The magnetic field dependence of the magnetization m, the topological charge |n|, and the total chirality χT z for J1 = 1 and J2 = 0.1 at θ = 0◦ . (b) Magnetic states at the center and corner icosahedrons in the unit cell of the bcc lattice for H > HM. (c) The energetically degenerate state with the magnetic state in (b) for H > HM. In (b) and (c), the number labels the rare-earth site on the icosahedron.

Fig. 15. (Color online) The anisotropy-angle θ dependence of χT y (left axis) and |JIC| (right axis) in the magnetic state shown in Fig. 5(f). The horizontal dashed line indicates χT µ = 0 (µ = x, y, z).
Fig. 14. (Color online) The non-collinear ferrimagnetic state for θ = 120◦ on the icosahedron denoted by the green-circle symbol in Fig. 5(e).
Fig. 13. The anisotropy-angle θ dependence of |χT| (left axis) and |JIC| (right axis) in the magnetic state shown in Fig. 5(d).

Fig. 5. (Color online) The ferrimagnetic orders on the icosahedrons located at the center and the corner of the bcc unit cell in the 1/1 AC. The alignment of the magnetic moments on the central icosahedron is viewed from the (111) direction.
